subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clearly labelled sections with a logical sequence (introduction, theory, methodology, analysis).
Good use of a contents page, numbered subsections and embedded tables/graphs.
Generally coherent development allowing reader to follow connections among data, differentiation, regression and modelling.
weaknesses: Missing page numbers in the Table of Contents and mis-labelled or duplicated figure captions hinder navigation.
Abrupt transitions between some subsections and occasional narrative back-tracking.
Excessive background explanation and repeated descriptions reduce conciseness; bibliography lacks formal citation style.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates clear organization and coherence (band 3) and mostly relevant content, but transitions, mis-labelling and unnecessary length prevent it from being exceptionally well-organized or highly concise. A score of 3 reflects strong structure with room for refinement.

strengths: Correct use of derivative notation (dT/dt, d^2T/dt^2) and largely consistent typesetting.
Effective integration of multiple representations (tables, graphs, regression curves, moving-average tables).
Adequate clarity in most explanations, supporting reader engagement with the mathematics.
weaknesses: Occasional notation slips (reusing aâ for two terms) and missing units or sub-scripts reduce precision.
Some graphs lack axis labels or have small fonts, impairing interpretability.
Long descriptive blocks mix procedure and commentary; a few algebraic errors cloud meaning.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly consistent, and representations effectively support comprehension (bands 3/4). However, minor notation errors and clarity issues prevent a top band, so a score of 3 accurately reflects the work.

strengths: Clear personal narrative linking the topic to the studentâs childhood experiences.
Evidence of independent thinking in selecting four cities and combining calculus with statistical methods.
Initiative shown by introducing a differential-equation model and comparing analytic/Euler solutions.
weaknesses: Approach uses standard methods rather than a highly innovative or novel framework.
Creativity is good but not exceptional; could explore more unique or original modelling ideas.
Opportunities exist to deepen personal insight or extend beyond textbook techniques.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission shows clear personal engagement, independent thinking and good initiative (all band 2), but does not reach the innovative depth required for band 3. A score of 2 reflects solid engagement with scope to expand creativity.

strengths: Includes basic observations linking seasonal gradients to warming trends.
Comments on statistical results (ranges, standard deviations, RÂ² values).
Notes agreement between analytic and Euler solutions.
weaknesses: Reflection is mainly descriptive with limited probing of model limitations or implications.
Lacks deeper critical analysis or exploration of future improvements (e.g. Fourier models, sensitivity analysis).
No substantial evaluation of outcomes or discussion of real-world applications and next steps.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three reflection strands fall in band 1, showing limited depth, basic analysis and minimal evaluation. A score of 1 accurately represents the elementary level of critical reflection present.

strengths: Relevant calculus, regression, differential equations and statistics are applied appropriately.
Demonstrates good understanding with mostly correct calculations and integration of methods.
Insightful comparison of analytic vs Euler solutions showing conceptual grasp.
weaknesses: Moderate depth of technique use; no higher-level methods (e.g. Fourier series) explored.
Minor algebraic slips and occasional omission of units/dimensional consistency.
Justification for some statistical choices (population vs sample SD) is lacking.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is fully commensurate with the SL syllabus and applied appropriately (band 3 across strands), but without the depth or sophistication for higher bands. A score of 3 reflects solid, accurate use of mathematics with minor errors.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within paragraphs, explanations are sometimes clear; however, long descriptive blocks mix procedure and commentary, and occasional algebraic errors cloud meaning. Hence logical flow and clarity are unevenâbetter than âlimitedâ but short of âgoodâ.

Calculations (finite differences, standard deviations, Euler iteration) are mostly correct; minor algebraic slips (polynomial form, average mis-type) do not undermine the results. Explanations show good conceptual grasp, though not fully thorough. Hence understanding is âgood with minor errorsâ, consistent with band 3.

Tables of raw data, derivative tables, scatter plots with regression curves and moving-average tables offer several representations. These are integrated with the text and help the reader follow the analysis. A few graphs lack axis labels or have small fonts, but overall use of representations is effective.

The report is divided into clearly-labelled sections (introduction, background theory, methodology, analysis) that proceed in a sensible sequence. Ideas generally follow one another without large logical gaps, enabling the reader to understand how the data, differentiation, regression and differential-equation modelling are connected. However, transitions between some subsections are abrupt and the narrative occasionally back-tracks, so the overall development is coherent rather than âhighlyâ coherent.

Introducing a differential-equation model and comparing analytic and Euler solutions, though elementary, reflects initiative beyond simply plotting data. The creativity is good but not exceptional, meriting the mid-level band.

The student comments on ranges, standard deviations and RÂ² values, providing elementary analysis. There is little probing of model limitations (e.g., suitability of quartic vs sinusoidal models) or parameter sensitivity. Thus only basic critical analysis is demonstrated.

Apart from noting that Hyderabad appears more stable or that Euler and analytic solutions agree closely, the student makes scant evaluation of how accurate or useful the models are for prediction or policy. Future work or implications are not explored, so evaluation remains limited.

Some reflective commentsâe.g., linking steep February-April gradients to pre-summer warming or noting monsoonal coolingâare present, but they are brief and descriptive. Depth of reflection is therefore limited.

Correct symbols are used for derivatives, moving averages and SD, and equations are typeset. Some slips occur (quartic model written twice with aâ and aâ, missing degree sign, incorrect sub-scripts) but the bulk of notation is appropriate and understandable, giving âmostly consistentâ rather than fully consistent use.

The personal narrative about growing up in northern India and the rationale for city selection provide a clear personal stamp. The investigation is guided by the studentâs interests, though the approach is not particularly novel, placing it in the âclear evidenceâ rather than âhighly innovativeâ category.

Most material is topical, but explanations of elementary concepts (e.g. what standard deviation means) and repeated descriptions of seasonal patterns add unnecessary length. The draft exceeds what is needed to answer the research question, so while relevance is mostly maintained, the exploration is not consistently concise.

Differentiation, numerical methods, quartic regression, standard deviation and moving averages are all relevant and correctly aligned with the research question. Mathematics is applied appropriately, but the depth of each technique is moderate rather than extensive, so the work is âappropriateâ rather than âeffectiveâ or âcomprehensiveâ.

Choosing four cities, devising a combined calculusâstatistics approach and performing hand-worked derivative examples show the studentâs own decisions rather than following a template. While methods are standard, the synthesis indicates significant independent thinking.

A contents page, numbered subsections and consistent headings demonstrate good organisation. Tables and graphs are embedded in the relevant places. Nevertheless, page references are missing, several figures are repeated or mis-labelled, and some lengthy blocks of text blur the distinction between body and conclusion, preventing the structure from reaching an âexceptionally well-organisedâ level.

All tools used are within the HL syllabus, yet none go substantially beyond (e.g., no Fourier analysis or multi-variable modelling). The mathematics therefore matches course level but does not push it, fitting the descriptor âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3).

annotations.comment: The Table of Contents lacks page numbers for many sections, making navigation difficult. Add accurate page references alongside each entry to improve the readerâs orientation and overall structure.

The introduction is overly long and includes general background that is not directly needed for the research question. Condense or remove repetitive descriptions to sharpen focus on your specific aim.

The personal narrative linking your childhood experiences in northern India to your research demonstrates clear independent thinking and personal engagement. It effectively grounds the investigation in real-world context.
The inline equation $$T = f(t)$$ is clear, but you could enhance clarity by specifying domain or units (e.g. months and Â°C). This detail would support precise mathematical communication.
The description of derivatives is pedagogically strong, with correct use of $$\frac{dT}{dt}$$ and $$\frac{d^2T}{dt^2}$$. Notation is mostly consistent and informative.
The polynomial model general form is well stated, but the inline list of coefficients merges terms without punctuation. Separate each $$a_i t^i$$ with commas to aid readability.
In the ODE $$\frac{dT}{dt}=k(T_{avg}-T)$$ you define k without units. Consider specifying that k has units monthâ»Â¹ to reinforce dimensional consistency.

The statistical section correctly presents $$\sigma=\sqrt{\frac{1}{N}\sum (T_i-\overline T)^2}$$, but omits justification for using population rather than sample standard deviation. Briefly explain your choice.
The raw data table is well embedded, but is titled and captioned as âFigure 1â. It should be labelled as a Table with a table number to maintain clarity.
Figure 2âs caption appears without the actual figure in that position. Ensure that each caption immediately follows its corresponding figure and is consistently numbered.
Some plotted graphs on page 9 lack axis labels and tick units, reducing interpretability. Add clear labels (e.g. Month, Temperature (Â°C)) to improve logical clarity.
The description of ranges (e.g. ârange equal to 17.1â) omits units. Always include Â°C when discussing temperature to maintain precision.

The algebraic sample for FebruaryâMarch on page 12 is correct, but the large table that follows is overwhelming. Summarise key values in text and move full tables to an appendix to improve conciseness.
Your narrative summarizing the derivative results provides basic observation, but lacks deeper critical analysis of what the temperature acceleration or deceleration signifies for climate modelling.
The quartic regression form on page 13 wrongly reuses coefficient $$a_3$$ for both cubic and quartic terms. Correct the notation to $$a_4 t^4$$ to avoid confusion.
Graph captions on pages 14â15 are duplicated and misnumbered (e.g. two âGraph 1â). Renumber and align each caption uniquely to maintain cohesion.
The evaluation of analytic vs Euler predictions is insightful, but lacks error quantification. Include percentage or absolute differences and comment on the modelâs reliability.
The large Euler vs analytic table on page 20 is comprehensive but visually dense. Highlight key deviations in commentary or plot to make insights more accessible.
The standard deviation for Dehradun is computed as $$\sigma\approx5.85Â°C$$, yet the sum of squared deviations is rounded from 410.79 to 410. Discuss any rounding impact on the final value.
The bibliography lists raw URLs without formal citations. Follow an academic style (e.g. APA) to present source details and access dates for completeness.
Your clear use of multiple representations (tables, graphs, equations) effectively communicates analysis to the reader. This enhances logical structure and reader engagement.
Consider reflecting on the limitations of polynomial fitting versus periodic (Fourier) models or including sensitivity analysis of model coefficients for deeper critical analysis.
The modeling approaches used are appropriate for the research question, but the work misses an exploration of future improvements (e.g. multi-variable models), limiting evaluation of outcomes.
The section on statistical variability offers meaningful insight, but lacks reflection on implicationsâsuch as how different SDs might inform regional climate vulnerability.
Moving average calculations are well detailed, but the extensive tables could be summarized in narrative form with selected highlights to improve conciseness.
Personal choice of four cities and combining calculus with statistical methods shows significant independent thinking and initiative in design.
Your use of both analytic and Eulerâs methods demonstrates a thorough understanding of numerical and exact solutions. The comparison is insightful and well communicated.

The investigation neatly integrates calculus, regression, differential equations, and statistics, showing relevant mathematics used appropriately. However, consider exploring a higherâlevel method (e.g. Fourier series) for deeper insight.
